title: incoming_quality_event_package_2026_07_11 (1)
normalized_content:
Incoming Quality Event Evidence Package
Organization: Aetheris BioPharma Private Limited
Event Arrival Date: 2026-07-11
Event Status: Newly discovered / open / evidence incomplete
Purpose: Initial evidence package for Helix (EvidenceOps) ingestion and AI-assisted
investigation
This package contains one new incoming quality event that does not appear in the previously
defined historical knowledge set. The package is intentionally incomplete and includes
conflicting statements, inconsistent timestamps, missing attachments, and unresolved gaps so
that an investigation platform can reconstruct what happened by correlating current evidence
with the Aetheris organization knowledge pack and operational records. Documentation and
record expectations in GMP environments emphasize that supporting records, linked
evidence, and event-specific details should be collected and preserved as part of deviation
and investigation handling.[cite:60][cite:65][cite:71]
Event overview
A newly reported issue concerns possible stopper/seal integrity concerns and visible residue
reports associated with FPP-2001 Injectra 1 g, manufactured at VISK on ML-ST-01, with
references to EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002, and EQ-UTL-002. The event surfaced through
an internal audit observation, a distributor complaint, and a manufacturing-floor review
within the same week, but the exact sequence remains unclear because the current evidence
contains date and narrative conflicts.[cite:60][cite:71]
Known associated objects at intake:
● Product: FPP-2001 Injectra 1 g
● Candidate batch: V20012607002
● Site: VISK
● Area: Sterile Block 1 / Secondary packaging interface
● Related equipment: EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-UTL-002
● Related employees: EMP-0303 M. Deepak, EMP-0204 T. Srinivas, EMP-0401 J. Jose,
EMP-0202 N. Lakshmi, EMP-0101 Meera Iyer
● Related supplier: SUP-003 SteriGlass International, SUP-008 PrintLabel Co
● Related SOPs: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0, VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0, VISK-QC-SOP-
QC-0510-R0, CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0304-R0, CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0

Document 1: Internal audit report excerpt
Document ID: VISK-QA-RPT-2026-0048-R0
Version: R0
Owner: Meera Iyer
Effective Date: 2026-07-10
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-UTL-002
Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0310-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-
QA-0113-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0101, EMP-0303, EMP-0204, EMP-0202
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Audit type: Focused internal walkthrough, sterile operations and batch-review readiness.
Auditor: Meera Iyer.
Audit date listed on cover page: 2026-07-09.
Observation logged in body: “During review on 10 July 2026, one retained shipping carton
and one in-process tray were noted with variable seal crimp appearance and one vial with
faint whitish residue near stopper edge.”
Finding summary:
● Batch packet for V20012607002 was not available in full at time of walkthrough; only
photocopied manufacturing pages 1–18 were provided.
● One in-process hold label on tray ST1-HLD-27 showed handwritten date “09/07/26”,
while packaging dispatch sheet referenced 08/07/26.
● No deviation number was visible in the batch packet copy at time of audit, despite verbal
mention from production that “QA already knows about one seal check issue.”
● Audit trail for environmental monitoring review was not attached to batch review packet.
Classification: Major observation pending escalation decision.
Missing/unclear items:
● Attachment A: tray photograph not attached.
● Attachment B: full batch genealogy missing.
● Attachment C: EM trend summary referenced but absent.
Document 2: Customer complaint intake

Document ID: VISK-CMP-2026-0034
Version: R0
Owner: N. Lakshmi
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002 (suspected), ML-PKG-02
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0304-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002 (reported by distributor), alternate handwritten note says
V20012607003
Related Employees: EMP-0202, EMP-0101
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Complaint source: GulfCare Distribution LLC, UAE.
Complaint received by: commercial inbox forwarded to QA.
Complaint date in email: 2026-07-10.
Complaint date entered in form: 2026-07-11.
Complaint text summary:
“Hospital pharmacist reported 2 vials in one shipper had slightly loose flip-off caps and one
vial showed a faint powder-like ring around stopper area. Product not administered. Photos
attached.”
Initial risk assessment: Major.
Units affected: “2 vials” in form body; “3 suspect vials” handwritten in margin.
Returned samples: Not yet received.
Photos: Referenced by distributor, but only one compressed image file reportedly received;
not attached to complaint form.
Inconsistencies:
● Batch number conflict between typed complaint and margin note.
● Complaint form says “no prior similar complaints found,” but historical knowledge
contains a prior visible particulate complaint for FPP-2001 in 2025, which may or may not
be relevant to current symptoms.
● The complaint does not distinguish residue from cosmetic stopper dust or dried product
deposit.
Document 3: Deviation report
Document ID: VISK-DEV-2026-0114
Version: R0

Owner: M. Deepak
Effective Date: 2026-07-10
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-ST-01-001
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0303, EMP-0204, EMP-0401, EMP-0202
Related Supplier: SUP-003
Event title: Irregular crimp appearance and possible stopper residue noted during post-fill
review.
Date/time discovered: 2026-07-09 18:40 per form header.
Narrative section states: “Observed on morning review of 10 July around 08:15.”
Description:
During post-fill review of retained samples from batch V20012607002, production and QA
observed at least one vial with uneven crimp skirt and visible light-colored deposit near the
stopper/cap interface. Additional review of an in-process tray reportedly identified one more
unit with cosmetic irregularity; number of suspect units not confirmed.
Immediate actions recorded:
● Segregated retained tray ST1-RET-09.
● Held finished pallet FP-07 pending QA decision.
● Informed QA and microbiology verbally.
Fields left blank:
● Preliminary impact to distributed units.
● Whether other batches on same equipment are affected.
● Reference to machine alarm log.
● Whether sample sent to QC.
Contradictory note:
At bottom of page, handwritten comment says “release already completed 09/07 by QA?” but
no release signature is attached.

Document 4: Machine log excerpt
Document ID: VISK-ENG-LOG-2026-0179
Version: R0
Owner: J. Jose
Effective Date: 2026-07-09
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-ENG-SOP-ENG-0607-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0401, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: None listed
Event log excerpt:
● 14:06 – “Minor feed-star adjustment due to intermittent cap seating variance.”
● 14:11 – alarm reset recorded.
● 14:13 – line resumed.
● 15:02 – “operator requested check on crimp profile sample.”
● 15:07 – “within visual acceptability per prod.”
● 16:48 – maintenance note states “guide rail contact marks seen on two empty vials during
challenge run.”
Missing attachments:
● Alarm screenshot not attached.
● No engineering sketch or measurement note for “cap seating variance.”
● No documented escalation to deviation in machine log.
Conflict with deviation:
Machine log suggests concern on 09 July afternoon, while deviation narrative emphasizes
discovery on 10 July morning.
Document 5: Equipment status label record
Document ID: VISK-QA-FRM-2026-ES-0042
Version: R0
Owner: T. Srinivas
Effective Date: 2026-07-10
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002

Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0309-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0101-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0204, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: None
Recorded equipment status: “Cleaned / Ready for Use” at 06:45 on 10 July.
Sticker observed in photograph description: “Under Observation / Do Not Use”
handwritten, time illegible.
Reviewer signature: missing.
Previous status label history: not attached.
Issue:
The documented equipment-status record conflicts with what was reportedly visible on the
line according to audit notes and photograph description.
Document 6: Calibration history excerpt
Document ID: VISK-CAL-2026-0018
Version: R0
Owner: J. Jose
Effective Date: 2026-06-28
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: PASH-ENG-SOP-ENG-0602-R0 (listed); expected site-specific sterile
engineering control not referenced
Related Product: FPP-2001; FPP-2003
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0401, EMP-0103
Related Supplier: None
Calibration excerpt:
● Fill volume verification module: Pass.
● Crimp-force verification: “within tolerance,” but raw values page missing.
● Vision sensor alignment check: N/A.
● Last calibration due date: 2027-06-27.
Inconsistencies:
● SOP reference points to a PASH engineering calibration SOP instead of a VISK record
pathway.

● Raw data page 3 of 4 missing.
● No indication whether crimp-force verification covered the exact setup parts used for the
current batch.
Document 7: Operator information sheet
Document ID: VISK-HR-FRM-2026-OPR-011
Version: R0
Owner: G. Patel
Effective Date: 2026-07-10
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0301-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0303, EMP-0204, EMP-0417, EMP-0418
Related Supplier: None
Shift personnel listed:
● EMP-0303 M. Deepak – Production Manager – oversight.
● EMP-0417 R. Chandra – Fill line operator – direct operation.
● EMP-0418 S. Fernandes – visual check assistant – temporary roster support.
● EMP-0204 T. Srinivas – EM review.
Issue:
EMP-0417 and EMP-0418 were not previously defined in the organization blueprint. Their
presence indicates either temporary/contract staffing not yet loaded into the knowledge pack
or a documentation inconsistency requiring resolution.
Additional concern:
Operator sheet says S. Fernandes was assigned to “cap inspection support,” but no training
excerpt for that person is included.

Document 9: Batch record excerpt
Document ID: VISK-BMR-FPP2001-2607-002
Version: R0
Owner: M. Deepak
Effective Date: 2026-07-08
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-UTL-002
Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0304-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0305-R0; VISK-ST-SOP-
ST-0309-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0303, EMP-0202, EMP-0204, EMP-0417, EMP-0418
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Batch excerpt content:
● Batch start date: 2026-07-08.
● Fill completion listed as 2026-07-09 13:55.

● Intervention section on page 12 shows one line entry: “cap path adjusted – 14:10,” but no
reason code selected.
● Line clearance checklist signed by production, QA checker box blank.
● Reconciliation page copied only partially; page footer shows “Page 19 of 26,” but only
pages 1–18 and 22 are present in packet.
● Final QA release page absent.
Potential inconsistency:
Packaging summary in margin indicates “120 cartons held,” while pallet sheet references “1
finished pallet + 8 shipper cases moved to quarantine.”
Document 10: Supplier documentation excerpt
Document ID: SUPC-SUP-003-2026-002
Version: R0
Owner: S. Naidu
Effective Date: 2026-07-01
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-001, EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0301-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001, FPP-2003
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0601, EMP-0101
Related Supplier: SUP-003
Supplier document type: Glass vial and stopper compatibility support file excerpt.
Contents present:
● Certificate of conformance for vial lot SG-V-44721.
● Packaging condition statement.
● Sterility packaging declaration.
Missing/unclear:
● Stopper component lot not clearly legible in scanned excerpt.
● Quality agreement appendix on change-notification timelines not attached.
● Document footer refers to “Attachment 5: siliconization process summary,” but attachment
missing.

Possible relevance:
Whitish ring/residue near stopper could be packaging-related, product-related, or handling-
related, but supplier documentation is incomplete and does not resolve source.
Document 11: Email conversation chain
Document ID: CORP-IT-RPT-2026-MSG-088
Version: R0
Owner: P. Singh
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-UTL-002
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0304-R0; CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0107-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002; one reply references V20012607003
Related Employees: EMP-0101, EMP-0202, EMP-0204, EMP-0303, EMP-0401
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Email excerpts:
2026-07-09 16:22 – From M. Deepak to Meera Iyer, J. Jose
“Noticed slight cap profile variation on end-of-run checks, engineering looked and felt okay
to continue. Holding one tray for QA look tomorrow.”
2026-07-10 08:41 – From N. Lakshmi to Meera Iyer
“Distributor from UAE asking if batch V20012607003 had any seal issue. They mention
powder ring on stopper. Could be transit abrasion?”
2026-07-10 09:05 – Reply from Meera Iyer
“Do not speculate. Confirm exact batch and stop any further dispatch of 07002 until we
review.”
2026-07-10 09:17 – From J. Jose to M. Deepak
“Logbook has star wheel tweak only, no confirmed mechanical fault.”
2026-07-10 17:58 – From T. Srinivas to Meera Iyer
“EM review looks normal except one Grade C prep room alert from 08 July, likely not
related.”

Inconsistencies:
● Batch referenced as 07003 in one email and 07002 elsewhere.
● Dispatch status unclear: one mail says stop further dispatch, suggesting some dispatch may
already have occurred.
● EM note says “looks normal” while internal audit says EM trend summary absent from
packet.
Document 12: Maintenance log excerpt
Document ID: VISK-MNT-2026-0029
Version: R0
Owner: J. Jose
Effective Date: 2026-07-09
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: VISK-ENG-SOP-ENG-0607-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0401, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: None
Log text:
“Adjusted cap feed guide and verified crimp sample visually after production request. No
parts replaced. No abnormal noise or jam observed.”
Missing details:
● No part number listed for adjusted guide.
● No before/after measurement values.
● No QA re-acceptance noted.
● No reference to whether change-part installation had been verified before run start.
Potential conflict:
Maintenance log calls the action a simple adjustment; machine log suggests intermittent
seating variance and contact marks during challenge run.

Document 13: Laboratory result excerpt
Document ID: VISK-QC-RPT-2026-0276-R0
Version: R0
Owner: T. Srinivas
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-QC-003
Related SOP: VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0509-R0; VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0510-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0204
Related Supplier: None
Sample submitted: “one suspect retained vial, external residue swab, stopper edge.”
Receipt time: 2026-07-11 10:05.
Condition on receipt: stopper/cap interface intact, visible faint ring.
Preliminary result: “No growth to date at 24 hr” handwritten.
Contradiction: report footer says “micro ID not initiated.”
Missing:
● No chemistry result for residue composition.
● No chain-of-custody signature from production.
● No sample photo attached.
Note:
A 24-hour no-growth note is not sufficient to rule out microbiological or non-microbiological
causes, and the package does not show a complete lab plan.
Document 14: Environmental monitoring excerpt
Document ID: VISK-EM-2026-0088
Version: R0
Owner: T. Srinivas
Effective Date: 2026-07-08
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-UTL-002
Related SOP: VISK-ST-SOP-ST-0302-R0; VISK-QC-SOP-QC-0510-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0204, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: None
EM excerpt:

● Grade A non-viable particles during fill: within spec.
● Grade B viable air at corridor B-01: within limit.
● Grade C prep room viable air: 8 cfu/m3; marked “alert” on worksheet.
● Glove print post-operation: one entry blank for Operator 2.
Conflicts / gaps:
● Email says Grade C prep-room alert “likely not related,” but no formal assessment
attached.
● One personnel monitoring field is blank, so operator linkage is incomplete.
● Trend chart not attached, though audit report references missing EM trend summary.
Document 15: Photograph descriptions
Document ID: VISK-QA-RPT-2026-PH-0012
Version: R0
Owner: Meera Iyer
Effective Date: 2026-07-10
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0102-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0101, EMP-0303
Related Supplier: SUP-003, SUP-008
Photo description 1:
Close-up of one Injectra 1 g vial on stainless tray. Crimp appears slightly asymmetric on left
side. A faint pale ring is visible at the stopper-metal interface. Image focus soft; batch
marking not readable.
Photo description 2:
Tray label ST1-HLD-27 shown beside four vials. Hold label date appears handwritten as
“09/07,” but time and initials are blurred.
Photo description 3:
Equipment status placard on EQ-ST-01-002 appears to show handwritten “Under
Observation,” but the preprinted underlying status card cannot be read fully.
Missing:

● Original image files absent.
● No scale or second-angle photograph.
● No photo proving shipper case condition from complaint site.
Document 16: Witness statements
Document ID: VISK-QA-FRM-2026-WS-0006
Version: R0
Owner: Meera Iyer
Effective Date: 2026-07-11
Related Equipment: EQ-ST-01-002, EQ-ST-01-001
Related SOP: CORP-QA-SOP-QA-0108-R0
Related Product: FPP-2001
Related Batch: V20012607002
Related Employees: EMP-0303, EMP-0204, EMP-0401, EMP-0417, EMP-0418
Related Supplier: None
Statement A – M. Deepak:
“Line ran normally overall. Engineering made a small cap-guide adjustment late in run after
one sample looked uneven, but I did not see any leaking vial. I asked for one tray to be held
for QA confirmation next day.”
Statement B – J. Jose:
“No confirmed machine fault found. We only fine-tuned the cap feed path. Two empty vials
showed contact marks during check, not filled units.”
Statement C – T. Srinivas:
“Micro/EM data available so far does not indicate aseptic control failure. One prep-room alert
exists but is not from Grade A/B filling zone. I have not yet reviewed any customer photo.”
Statement D – R. Chandra:
“One vial looked different near the cap after restart. I informed supervisor. Not sure whether
it was before or after guide adjustment.”
Statement E – S. Fernandes:
“I was told to help visually separate unusual-looking units. I am not sure how many were
removed because the tray was moved before I counted.”

Inconsistencies:
● Number of suspect units varies by witness.
● Timing relative to restart/adjustment is unclear.
● One witness was involved in visual segregation without confirmed training completion in
the attached excerpt.
Evidence gaps summary
The package is materially incomplete in several areas that a real investigation would likely
need to resolve:[cite:60][cite:71]
● Exact batch identity is inconsistent between 07002 and 07003 in some records.
● Full batch record and QA release page are missing.
● Complaint photographs and returned samples have not been fully secured.
● Equipment status history is contradictory.
● Calibration raw data page is missing.
● Supplier stopper/packaging support attachments are incomplete.
● Personnel qualification for at least one involved person is not confirmed.
● EM assessment is partial and lacks formal relation-to-batch determination.
● Laboratory work is preliminary and does not establish composition or source of residue.
● No final root cause or product impact conclusion is available.
Intake correlation cues for AI investigation
This event should be correlated against prior Aetheris knowledge for the following themes:
● Prior sterile packaging and cosmetic/particulate-type complaint history for FPP-2001.
● Previous label and packaging control issues involving SUP-008.
● Prior intervention and hold-time documentation weaknesses at VISK.
● Equipment history for EQ-ST-01-002 including maintenance, calibration, and validation
state.
● Training completeness and operator authorization on sterile lines.
● EM trends for VISK sterile support and prep areas.[cite:64][cite:69][cite:71]
Current disposition

Recommended intake status for Helix: Open event / evidence package incomplete / triage
for formal investigation.
Root cause: Not determined.
CAPA: Not initiated in this package.
Quality decision: Hold / assess / correlate with organization knowledge.
